qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Command injection via external tool path configuration permits arbitrary command execution
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes command injection through unsanitized external tool paths in ProcessBuilder (CWE-78). The advisory describes the REST login endpoint exposing credentials via HTTP GET query strings (CWE-598). The root cause, vulnerable code (BaseConverter/ProcessHelper vs REST login endpoint), attack vector (admin config manipulation vs passive credential exposure), and CWE class are entirely different.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding matches the advisory-described vulnerability. The advisory (GHSA-gcvm-c75m-h4p4, CVE-2026-34020) describes CWE-598: the REST login endpoint uses HTTP GET with username and password as query parameters, exposing sensitive credentials in URLs that can be logged, cached, or leaked via referrer headers. The only qualifying finding describes command injection through unsanitized external tool paths passed to ProcessBuilder, which is a fundamentally different vulnerability class (CWE-78), different root cause, different vulnerable code, and different attack surface.
evidence: Advisory CWE-598: Use of GET Request Method With Sensitive Query Strings in REST login endpoint
Finding CWE-78 type: Command injection via tool path configuration in ProcessBuilder
Advisory root cause: credentials passed as GET query parameters instead of POST body
Finding root cause: tool paths retrieved from DB and passed unsanitized to ProcessBuilder
Advisory affected code: REST login endpoint
Finding affected code: BaseConverter.java, ProcessHelper.java, ConfigForm.java
